THIS IS A LATE REPORT FOR THE STORM THAT STRUCK THE ELLENDALE AREA DURING THE EARLY MORNING HOURS OF JUNE 14. EXTENSIVE DAMAGE WAS REPORTED THROUGHOUT THE CITY OF ELLENDALE AND SURROUNDING AREAS. IN ELLENDALE, MANY HARDWOOD TREES WERE SNAPPED AND OTHERS UPROOTED, SOME FALLING ONTO HOMES. A HOME HAD ALMOST ALL THE SHINGLES AND MUCH OF THE SHEATHING TORN OFF THE ROOF, AND THE GARAGE DOOR WAS COLLAPSED INWARD. SOME POWER POLES WERE BROKEN IN TOWN, AND SEVERAL POWER SERVICE LINES TO HOMES WERE TORN. SOUTH OF ELLENDALE TO THE SOUTH DAKOTA STATE LINE, MULTIPLE POWER POLES WERE SNAPPED. AT THE ELLENDALE CEMETERY MULTIPLE HARDWOOD AND SOFTWOOD TREES WERE SNAPPED AND OTHERS WERE UPROOTED. BASED UPON THE EXTENSIVE DAMAGE, WIND SPEEDS ARE ESTIMATED TO BE AROUND 100 MPH IN THE CITY OF ELLENDALE, AND 110 MPH TO THE EAST AND TO THE SOUTH OF ELLENDALE. THE DICKEY COUNTY EMERGENCY MANAGER WAS INSTRUMENTAL IN ASSISTING THE NWS WHILE THE STORMS WERE ONGOING AND IN THE DAYS FOLLOWING.

THIS IS A LATE REPORT FOR THE STORM THAT STRUCK THE FAIRFIELD AREA DURING THE VERY EARLY MORNING HOURS OF JULY 10. EXTENSIVE DAMAGE WAS REPORTED THROUGHOUT THE COMMUNITY OF FAIRFIELD AND SURROUNDING AREAS, PARTICULARLY TO THE WEST. APPROXIMATELY 100 POWER POLES WERE SNAPPED THROUGHOUT THE AREA. A PAD MOUNTED ELECTRICAL TRANSFORMER WEIGHING APPROXIMATELY TWO TONS WAS BLOWN OVER ABOUT EIGHT MILES WEST OF FAIRFIELD. SIX MILES WEST OF FAIRFIELD, A 120 FOOT COMMUNICATIONS TOWER BLEW OVER, AND AN ADJACENT SMALL BUILDING WAS DEMOLISHED. CLOSER TO AND IN THE COMMUNITY OF FAIRFIELD, A QUONSET HUT WAS BLOWN APPROXIMATELY 150 YARDS, AND A ROOF ON A HOME WAS SEVERELY DAMAGED. MULTIPLE TREES IN THE AREA WERE UPROOTED OR SNAPPED, AND MULTIPLE STOCK TRAILERS WERE FLIPPED OVER. BASED UPON THE EXTENSIVE DAMAGE, WIND SPEEDS ARE ESTIMATED TO BE AROUND 120 MPH WITH THIS STORM. GREAT APPRECIATION IS EXTENDED TO THE BILLINGS COUNTY EMERGENCY MANAGEMENT TEAM, THE LOCAL POWER COOPERATIVE, AND LOCAL MEMBERS OF THE PUBLIC FOR GATHERING AND SHARING INFORMATION WITH THE NATIONAL WEATHER SERVICE.
TIME ESTIMATED FROM RADAR. REPORT OF A 150 FOOT TALL TOWER THAT WAS DOWNED BY STRONG WINDS. THERE WAS ALSO A 10 FOOT BY 12 FOOT BUILDING WITH A LOT OF WEIGHT IN IT THAT WAS COMPLETELY DEMOLISHED.

FROM JULY 18 - BASED ON INFORMATION PROVIDED BY THE WILLIAMS COUNTY EMERGENCY MANAGER AND AREA RESIDENTS, INCLUDING MANY PHOTOS AND IN SPEAKING WITH THEM, AND RADAR DATA, IT WAS DETERMINED THAT THUNDERSTORM WINDS AROUND 95 MPH, AND NOT A TORNADO, IMPACTED THE WILDROSE AREA LAST NIGHT FOR ABOUT 10 MINUTES, FROM 1015 TO 1025 PM. DAMAGE OCCURRED TO OUTBUILDINGS AND TREES. SHEET METAL WALLS WERE BLOWN INTO VEHICLES, CAMPERS AND MOTORHOMES, DAMAGING THEM. SOME TREES WERE UPROOTED BUT MORE IMPORTANTLY A FEW LARGE DIAMETER TREE TRUNKS WERE SNAPPED. THIS SNAPPING OF LARGE TREE TRUNKS LED TO THE 95 MPH SPEED ESTIMATE. NWS THANKS EM MIKE SMITH FOR HIS ASSISTANCE IN GATHERING THIS STORM DATA. A SHOUT-OUT ALSO TO THOSE WHO POSTED DAMAGE PHOTOS TO SOCIAL MEDIA AND MAKING THOSE ACCESSIBLE TO THE NWS.

EM AND PUBLIC REPORT AND PICTURES OF SIGNIFICANT DAMAGE IN WILDROSE. DAMAGE INCLUDES LOTS OF LARGE TREES UPROOTED AND BLOCKING STREETS, MULTIPLE OPEN STAND BUILDINGS DESTROYED, AT LEAST ONE ROOF GONE, AND POWER OUT TO THE ENTIRE TOWN. EM NOTED EVERYTHING WAS BLOWN IN ONE DIRECTION, INDICATING DAMAGE WAS CAUSED BY STRAIGHT LINE WINDS.

BASED ON INFORMATION PROVIDED BY THE ROLETTE COUNTY EMERGENCY MANAGER, INCLUDING PHOTOS AND IN SPEAKING WITH HIM, AND RADAR DATA, IT WAS DETERMINED THAT THUNDERSTORM WINDS AROUND 75 MPH, AND NOT A TORNADO, IMPACTED THIS AREA FOR ABOUT 10 MINUTES, FROM 342 AM TO 352 AM THIS MORNING. DAMAGE OCCURRED TO TWO PROPERTIES ALONG BIA ROAD 6 AND INCLUDED PARTIAL LOSS OF ROOF MATERIALS ON A HOME, DAMAGE TO OUTBUILDINGS AND LAWN DECORATIONS, AND BROKEN TREE BRANCHES. WIND WAS MEASURED AT 67 MPH ABOUT 4 MILES SOUTHWEST OF BELCOURT AT 338 AM, AND LATER 64 MPH WIND WAS MEASURED AT THE ROLLA AIRPORT. NWS THANKS EM MIKE STEWART FOR HIS ASSISTANCE IN GATHERING THIS STORM DATA.
AWOS STATION K06D ROLLA ND AWOS.

LATE REPORT FROM JULY 21. HAIL FELL FOR A FEW MINUTES. 0.70 INCHES OF RAIN. THIS SEVERE THUNDERSTORM TRACKED SOUTHEAST, MISSING MONTPELIER BY A FEW MILES TO THE EAST, AND THEN CROSSED INTO BARNES COUNTY, JUST NORTH OF HIGHWAY 46, THEN INTO NORTHEASTERN LAMOURE COUNTY. REPORTS WERE THAT CROPS WERE DAMAGED BUT THE EXTENT OF THE DAMAGE WAS NOT KNOW AS OF THE TIME THIS REPORT IS BEING SENT.

HAIL RANGED IN DIAMETER FROM HALF DOLLAR TO GOLF BALL SIZE, WITH MOST OF IT BEING PING PONG BALL SIZE. THE HAIL HAD A WHITE CORE ABOUT 3/4 INCH IN DIAMETER WITH THE REST OF THE STONE AROUND IT BEING CLEAR. VERY FEW TOTAL HAILSTONES, BUT THE ONES THAT DID FALL WERE LARGE. NO RAIN. DURATION OF THE HAIL WAS 5 MINUTES.
HAIL IS COATING THE GROUND MOSTLY PEA SIZE WITH SOME QUARTERS AND PING PONG SIZE HAIL.

REPORT FROM MONDAY AUGUST 15. SECOND OF THREE TORNADOES IN THIS AREA. TORNADO MOVED SOUTH AND ENDED 4 MILES SOUTH OF RUSO AT 535 PM. HARDWOOD TREES SNAPPED AND ANIMAL SHELTER DESTROYED SOON AFTER IT DEVELOPED. DAMAGE RATING EF-1 WITH WINDS FROM THAT ESTIMATED AT ABOUT 110 MPH.
PUBLIC REPORT OF A TORNADO TOUCHDOWN.

REPORT FROM MONDAY AUGUST 15. THIRD OF THREE TORNADOES IN THIS AREA. THIS THIRD TORNADO STARTED WHILE THE SECOND ONE WAS ONGOING. THE TWO TORNADOES WERE ONGOING TOGETHER FOR 5 MINUTES (530 PM TO 535 PM). TORNADO MOVED SOUTH AND ENDED 8 MILES SOUTH OF RUSO AT 550 PM. 6 MILES SOUTH OF RUSO THREE SMALL WOODEN GRANARIES WERE DESTROYED, FARM EQUIPMENT WAS DAMAGED INCLUDING THE FLIPPING OF A LARGE SEED CART ATTACHED TO AN AIR SEEDER, TOSSED HAY BALES AND FLIPPED ANHYDROUS TANKS. DAMAGE RATING EF-2 WITH WINDS FROM THAT ESTIMATED AT ABOUT 120 MPH.
TORNADO TOUCHDOWN AND THEN LIFTED WITHIN A FEW MINUTES.
PUBLIC REPORT OF ANOTHER TORNADO ON THE GROUND.
2 E WATFORD CITY NDAWN.

EXTENSIVE CROP DAMAGE FROM JUST EAST OF PARSHALL, MOUNTRAIL COUNTY, TO THE SOUTHEAST TO NEAR ROSEGLEN, MCLEAN COUNTY. THIS IS ABOUT AN 18 MILE LONG SWATH. DAMAGE TO LENTILS, WHEAT, CANOLA AND CORN. CORN STALKS STRIPPED AND CORN ITSELF DAMAGED, LIKELY DUE TO WIND DRIVEN HAIL. WIND MEASURED 10 SOUTH PLAZA WAS 76 MPH.
VARIOUS SIZES OF HAIL WITH THE BIGGEST AT QUARTER SIZE. HEAVY RAIN. DID NOT MEASURE IT YET.
